memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with minimal stress, but I'm tired. Attending the leisure event seems like a good way to relax before tackling the term project later.
Working 2 hours reduced future stress but only moved the project to 0.28 completion. With afternoon slipping and high remaining deadlines, I must prioritize finishing work over leisure.
After two hours of work, my progress jumped to 0.47, yet I still feel tired with only three days left on the term project.
Progress increased to 0.61 after two hours, but with only three days left and high stress, I must continue working immediately.
Worked 2 hours, progress rose to 0.70 in three days. Still need more time despite low energy and high stress.
I failed an instant chore while barely advancing my term project. With zero energy and high stress, I cannot sustain this pace.
Worked 1.5 hours, progress rose to 0.84 in three days. Despite low energy and high stress, I am still barely advancing.
After sleeping 4 hours, my energy is now 4.8 with zero sleep debt, but stress remains high and progress is still slow.
The term project is now complete. With full energy and no sleep debt, I can finally focus on finishing the chore without stress.
Despite recovering energy and completing the term project, progress on the trivial chore remains minimal after 0.5 hours.
The term project is finished, yet the trivial chore still shows zero progress despite high energy and no sleep debt.
After 1 hour, the trivial chore's progress only increased by 0.03, confirming previous observations that this task is exceptionally slow despite my high energy and lack of stress.
